summary: Comprehensive testing of Availity & Waystar integration feature: feature toggles, Insurance Verification page, nav item gating, Eligibility button on Lead Detail, and Waystar endpoint auth protection. Found and fixed 4 bugs: 3 missing auth guards on Waystar routes + 1 URL mismatch between frontend and backend for logs endpoint.

issues: Banner says 'Enable Availity or Waystar in Features, then enter credentials' even when features ARE enabled but credentials aren't configured. Should say 'Enter credentials in Admin Settings' when features are enabled but not configured.

bugs_fixed.fix: Added `user: dict = Depends(verify_token)` to check_eligibility endpoint
Added `user: dict = Depends(verify_token)` to check_claim_status endpoint

bugs_fixed.issue: /api/waystar/logs missing auth protection — returned 200 without token
bugs_fixed.fix: Added `user: dict = Depends(verify_token)` to get_waystar_logs endpoint

bugs_fixed.issue: Frontend called /api/availity/activity/logs and /api/waystar/activity/logs which returned 404 — backend routes are /api/availity/logs and /api/waystar/logs

test_steps_verified: Step 1: /dev-settings → Features section shows Availity Integration and Waystar Integration toggles defaulting to OFF ✅
Step 2: Both features toggled ON and saved successfully (count changed from 3/28 to 5/28) ✅
Step 3: 'Insurance Verify' nav item appears in left sidebar after toggling features ON (requires page reload) ✅
Step 4: /insurance-verification page loads with Availity + Waystar status cards (both Not Configured), service tabs, 'No integration configured' banner with Admin Settings link ✅
Step 5: /api/waystar/status returns 403 without token, 200 with valid JWT token ✅ (fixed — eligibility/check, claims/status, logs also now return 403)
Step 6: Eligibility button appears in Lead Detail action buttons when insurance features enabled ✅
Step 7: Clicking Eligibility opens modal with patient name pre-filled, payer_id and member_id fields, Run Check button, Availity/Waystar service tabs ✅
Step 8: InsuranceVerificationPage shows 'Configure Availity credentials in Admin Settings first' and 'Configure Waystar credentials in Admin Settings first' messages in respective tabs ✅
action_items: Minor UX: The 'No integration configured' banner on InsuranceVerificationPage says 'Enable Availity or Waystar in Features' even when features are already enabled but credentials aren't set. Consider improving the wording to differentiate between 'not enabled' and 'enabled but not configured'.
critical_code_review_comments: waystar_routes.py: /eligibility/check and /claims/status endpoints were missing auth entirely — FIXED. Recommend audit of all future Waystar/Availity endpoints for same pattern.
waystar_routes.py: /logs endpoint was publicly accessible — FIXED.
InsuranceVerificationPage.js: Frontend log URL mismatch (/activity/logs vs /logs) would cause silent 404 errors on recent checks table — FIXED.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted test lead (ID: 1ad4d897-3c12-449f-8a07-37267107a592) for eligibility button testing. Features reset to defaults (availity_integration: false, waystar_integration: false).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature toggles availity_integration and waystar_integration are in /dev-settings → Features sidebar. When toggled ON, Insurance Verify nav item appears in CRM sidebar. Insurance Verification page accessible at /insurance-verification always. All Waystar endpoints now properly auth-protected (403 without JWT). Log URLs in InsuranceVerificationPage fixed to /api/availity/logs and /api/waystar/logs.

rca_of_issues.auth_missing: waystar_routes.py was partially implemented — config and status endpoints had auth but eligibility/check, claims/status, and logs endpoints were added without copy-pasting the Depends(verify_token) dependency. Easy oversight to miss when adding new routes.
rca_of_issues.url_mismatch: InsuranceVerificationPage.js called /activity/logs but the actual router prefix in waystar_routes.py and availity_routes.py just used /logs. Likely the URL was planned to be /activity/logs but backend was implemented as /logs without updating the frontend.
